and we are supposed to interpret your description how? Guess

"Thunderbird suddenly stopped formatting the body of my emails correctly. "

What does that mean? What is correctly? Formatting them when you write them, when you get them or what?

Have you tried repairing the folder if it is an incoming mail issue. (right click, select properties and then the repair button)Sometime indexed get messed up (usually by antivirus programs) but unformatted and often unrelated raw HTML can be the result of indexing issues.

For compose issues try holding the shift key when opening the compose window (that forces text only if using HTML and vise versa and is a diagnostic tool.)

content:
Hi drlarry, What you're describing sounds like signatures.

From the article:

"Signatures" are blocks of text that are automatically appended to every message that you send (including both new messages and replies to incoming messages). They are generally used to provide additional contact information, legal terms or some other boilerplate information that is relevant to every email.

For more information and instructions on how to add them, please check: Signatures
creator: Fylvara

content:
Yes, it is. Those are known as "signatures". Here's an article about them and how to set them up:

https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/signatures
creator: rednoise

content: If I may offer a suggestion, if your intent is that the recipient focus on content and not on font, use font 'variable=width' and size medium in your settings>composition pane. That setting ensures that your message will be presented in the recipient's font and font size.. As you stated, the other settings in latin and other writing systems are for you, not the recipient. For your signature, also do not use a font or font size. That ensures that your sig will be consistent to recipient.
creator: davidsk

content:
Thanks to Ed & sfhowes for your helpful replies. It was "sfhowes" solution that solved the problem. I went onto the outlook website, clicked the gear setting icon> Mail>Forwarding and IMAP> Pop and IMAP and selected LET DEVICES AND APPS USE IMAP.

When you first go to Forwarding and IMAP, you have to click a button that says sign in to make changes. I clicked on that and nothing happened. It turns out, I had a "Popup blocker extension" enabled in EDGE. It was preventing the sign-in password popup from opening which prevented me from seeing the "Let Devices and Apps use Imap" toggle switch. I turned off the popup blocker, and the security password popup appeared. I signed in, and that allowed the "Let Devices and apps use Imap" to appear. Toggle on.

So thanks sfhowes, and I hope this detailed solution helps others! This is why I love the internet...people helping people. You guys are great.
